map1_cm4ai.headline: 22% of high-confidence edges fail conformal FDR at q=0.10

map2_hostpathogen.confidence_axis: pDockQ (CPU from ranked-0 model; ipTM not deposited)
map2_hostpathogen.reason: positives-only, pre-thresholded on confidence; no exchangeable null; pDockQ saturated (no ranking information left in released set)

finding: Auditability is a property of the data RELEASE, not the map. A distribution-free conformal FDR audit requires an exchangeable null (a matched negative/decoy set run through the same pipeline). CM4AI ships 1,788 native random-pair decoys and is fully auditable; the host-pathogen deposit is positives-only with a saturated confidence axis and cannot be audited post-hoc without GPU-scale decoy folding. Implication: AI-interactome releases should ship their negative controls to enable independent error-rate audit.
